assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been recognised as a receivable and creates foreign exchange risk in the consolidated financial statements.
The question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a fair value hedge as a hedge of the exposure to changes in fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a cash flow hedge as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk associated with recognised items or forecast transactions that could affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct foreign currency hedge accounting model in consolidated financial statements.
